---
title: "Slow window draging with dual graphic cards"
date: 2008-04-01
forum: Multimedia &amp; Video
---

### Post by fastae on 2008-04-01
I'm having problem with slow scrolling and dragging window.

I have two graphic cards

GeForce FX5200 uses RGB cable
ATi uses DVI cable

here's my xorg.conf
```


# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"kr"
	Option		"XkbVariant"	"kr104"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"nvidiapci"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:10:0"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"DellR"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-130
	VertRefresh	50-160
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"ScreenR"
	Device		"nvidiapci"
	Monitor		"DellR"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

##############SECOND DEVICE####################
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:2:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"SP2208WFPSILVER"
	Option		"DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"ScreenL"
	Device		"ATI"
	Monitor		"SP2208WFPSILVER"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1680x1050" "1280x1024" "800x640"
		viewport 0 0
	EndSubSection
EndSection

##########################################


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"ScreenR" 0 0
	Screen		"ScreenL" LeftOf "ScreenR"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	Option		"Xinerema" "on"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

```

fgrlinfo gives me
```

fastae@fastae-desktop:~$ fglrxinfo 
NVIDIA: Direct rendering failed; attempting indirect rendering.
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: NVIDIA Corporation
OpenGL renderer string: GeForce FX 5200/PCI/SSE2
OpenGL version string: 2.1.1 NVIDIA 100.14.19

```

The NVIDIA monitor side is perfectly fine.  The problem occurs only with the ATi side. I installed newest ATi driver, but whenever I tried aticonfig --initial, system crashes, so I had manually recover the xorg.conf file.  Also, Totem only works for NVIDIA side, i had to drag the window to NVIDIA side to watch a movie~lol

Can anyone give me some advices?  I'm on Gutsy, I've been using like this for a while without fixing it, but I just can't stand today~~
